introSection: Jumbo Jet is a sit-down roller coaster built by German manufacturer Schwarzkopf currently located at Amigoland in Grau-du-Roi, Occitanie, France. It is currently owned by French showman William Bouvier.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride was originally built in Germany and first travelled with German showmen Schippers and v. d. Ville.

It was later sold to Willy Miller's Continental Park Attractions who in turn leased it to Great Adventure in Jackson, New Jersey, USA during 1975. The ride never opened to the public and was then removed after the same year. It was built as part of the new Fun Fair section of the park.

The ride eventually was later sold to Conklin Shows of Canada, which operated it around 1982 as part of the Canadian National Exhibition. Along with this, it is also believed to have travelled Canada with them when the fair was closed.

The ride was later sold to French showman William Bouvier who reopened it in 1989 at Amigoland in France.

fullText:
Jumbo Jet is a sit-down roller coaster built by German manufacturer Schwarzkopf currently located at Amigoland in Grau-du-Roi, Occitanie, France. It is currently owned by French showman William Bouvier.

The ride was originally built in Germany and first travelled with German showmen Schippers and v. d. Ville.

It was later sold to Willy Miller's Continental Park Attractions who in turn leased it to Great Adventure in Jackson, New Jersey, USA during 1975. The ride never opened to the public and was then removed after the same year. It was built as part of the new Fun Fair section of the park.

The ride eventually was later sold to Conklin Shows of Canada, which operated it around 1982 as part of the Canadian National Exhibition. Along with this, it is also believed to have travelled Canada with them when the fair was closed.

The ride was later sold to French showman William Bouvier who reopened it in 1989 at Amigoland in France.
